summary: Clear soundboard recording captures a stellar 1972 performance highlighted by a thunderous 'Playing in the Band' jam and an epic The Other One/Black Peter sandwich.
review: The SBD source delivers clean, well‑balanced audio that lets the band’s tight interplay shine.  Highlights include a blazing 'Playin'' intro, Keith’s Rhodes work and pedal wizardry on The Other One, and a memorable Too > Black Peter > Too sandwich, while Donna’s vocal quirks spark debate but don’t detract from the overall brilliance.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show 4‑5 stars and praise the musical peaks; recording quality is consistently described as good to nice, yielding an overall strong combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Nice, clear sound quality with crisp instrument separation; no major drop‑outs or muddy sections reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows through several classic sandwiches (Too > Black Peter > Too, The Other One > Black Peter > The Other One) and a spacey second half, delivering strong jams and memorable transitions.

band_member_comments.Phil: Several reviewers note Phil dropping “bombs” and delivering beautiful harmonies early in the set.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob’s guitar intro on 'Playing in the Band' is singled out as powerful and unusually forward‑in‑the‑mix.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s Rhodes work is praised, and his wa‑wa pedal shines during The Other One jam.
